title: Energy for America’s Economic Future Act
short_title: Energy for America’s Economic Future Act
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
plain_summary: This bill creates a special fund to help pay off the government's debt. Every three months, the government will put 25% of the money it makes from oil and gas leases and artificial intelligence activities into this fund. The money in the fund can only be used to pay back money the government owes. This includes money from companies that bid on oil and gas leases, as well as payments they make over time like royalties and fees.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Energy for America's Economic Future Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Debt Reduction Fund
sections.text: This section creates a new fund to help pay down the national debt. The Fund means the Debt Reduction Fund that this law creates. The Secretary means the Secretary of the Treasury, who is in charge of the government's money. Total revenue means all the money the government collects when companies buy leases to drill for oil and gas on federal land, plus all the payments those companies make over time including royalties, rent, and fees. The government will create a special account in the Treasury called the Debt Reduction Fund. Starting one hundred days after this law passes, every three months the government must put money into this fund. Twenty five percent of all money collected from oil and gas lease sales on federal land and offshore areas must go into the fund. This includes leases under several federal laws that govern mineral rights. Twenty five percent of all money collected from activities related to Executive Order fourteen one four one about artificial intelligence infrastructure must also go into the fund. All money put into this fund can only be used to pay down the federal debt. Every three months, the Secretary of the Treasury must use all the money in the fund to buy back Treasury securities that are owned by the public or pay off other government debt. Starting one year after this law passes and every three months after that, the Secretary must send Congress a report. The report must show how much money was put into the fund and used to pay down debt during that period. It must list which Treasury securities or other debts were paid off and how much the total federal debt went down.
